data.title: Argenx, Halozyme win FDA nod for Vyvgart Hytrulo prefilled syringe
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has approved Dutch biotech argenx’s (NASDAQ:ARGX) (OTCPK:ARGNF) autoimmune disease drug Vyvgart Hytrulo, developed with Halozyme Therapeutics (NASDAQ:HALO), as a prefilled syringe for self-injection.

The treatment was approved for adults suffering from two autoimmune disorders: those with generalized myasthenia gravis (who are positive for anti-acetylcholine receptor antibodies) and chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP).

The antibody drug will therefore be indicated as a 20-to-30-second subcutaneous injection to be administered by a patient, caregiver, or healthcare professional, the companies said.

The approval adds the third administration option for Vyvgart Hytrulo, which was first approved by the FDA in June 2023 for gMG and later expanded to cover CIDP in June 2024.

“Today’s FDA approval provides a new self-injection option across both approved indications in the U.S. that is designed for patients who seek more independence with their treatment,” noted ARGX’s medical chief Luc Truyen.

data.title: Wells Fargo in charts: Q1 Consumer Banking and Lending revenue down Y/Y
Driven Brands makes the strategic decision to sell its U.S. car wash business
data.description:
Driven Brands Holdings (NASDAQ:DRVN) announced that it closed on the sale of its U.S. car wash business to Express Wash Operations.

Under the terms of the agreement, Whistle paid Driven Brands (NASDAQ:DRVN) approximately $255 million in cash and delivered a negotiable interest-bearing seller note in the principal amount of $130 million. Final proceeds are subject to customary post-closing adjustments and payment of transaction-related fees and expenses. 

Driven Brands (DRVN) said cash proceeds from the transaction will be used to pay down debt as it works to achieve 3X or less net leverage by the end of 2026. In FY25, Driven Brands (DRVN) plans to report the results of the U.S. car wash business as discontinued operations.

Looking ahead, the Charlotte-based company expects the asset sale to mark an important milestone by enabling it to focus on the Take 5 Oil Change business. CEO Jonathan Fitzpatrick noted that Driven Brands'(DRVN) is now underpinned by stable, cash-generating franchise businesses. "We are confident this disposition will better position the company to execute on our strategic initiatives," he noted.

In FY25, Driven Brands (DRVN) plans to report the results of the U.S. car wash business as discontinued operations.

data.title: Fastenal GAAP EPS of $0.52 in-line, revenue of $1.96B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Fastenal press release (NASDAQ:FAST): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.52 in-line.
  • Revenue of $1.96B (+3.7% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Net sales increased $64.3, or 3.4%, in the first quarter of 2025 when compared to the first quarter of 2024. There was one less selling day in the first quarter of 2025 relative to the prior year period and, taking this into consideration, our net daily sales increased 5.0% in the first quarter of 2025 compared to the first quarter of 2024.

data.title: JD.com to set up $27B fund to boost exporters domestic sales as trade war heats up - report
data.description:
China's JD.com (NASDAQ:JD) will reportedly spend $27.B to set up a fund to boost the country's exporters to sell their products in the domestic market over the next year as the U.S.-China trade war heats up. 

China has announced an increase in tariffs on U.S. goods to 125%, up from the previous rate of 84%, effective April 12, and stated it will disregard any additional tariff hikes announced by Washington moving forward.

The company will send its staff to Chinese companies that deal in foreign trade and directly buy their "high quality products" and will look to set up an area on its e-commerce site to sell these products, Reuters reported.

Amidst U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs, Chinese exporters are expected to see losses, however, programs aimed at helping the exporters could mitigate some of the hit from reduced sales. 

 The White House on Thursday clarified that the cumulative tariff on China-produced goods coming into the U.S. will total 145%.

Trump's tariff announcements have put pressure on JD.com, dragging its shares down nearly 13% over the past month.

data.title: Wells Fargo Non-GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.05, revenue of $20.15B misses by $610M
data.description:
• Wells Fargo press release (NYSE:WFC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $20.15B (-3.4% Y/Y) misses by $610M.
  • Net interest income decreased 6%, driven by the impact of lower interest rates on floating rate assets, deposit mix and pricing changes, lower loan balances, and one fewer day in the quarter, partially offset by lower market funding
  • Provision for credit losses of $932M included a decrease in the allowance for credit losses, reflecting a lower allowance for commercial real estate loans on lower loan balances, partially offset by a higher allowance for commercial and industrial loans

data.title: JPMorgan Non-GAAP EPS of $4.91 beats by $0.27, revenue of $46B beats by $1.86B
data.description:
• JPMorgan press release (NYSE:JPM): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.91 beats by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $46B (+9.7% Y/Y) beats by $1.86B.
  •  Book value per share of $119.24, up 12% YoY; tangible book value per share2 of $100.36, up 13% YoY
  • Basel III common equity Tier 1 capital of $280 billion, Standardized ratio of 15.4% and Advanced ratio of 15.5%
  • Assets under management were $4.1 trillion, and client assets were $6.0 trillion, each up 15%, driven by continued net inflows and higher market levels.
  • The provision for credit losses was $3.3 billion. Net charge-offs were $2.3 billion, up $376 million, predominantly driven by Card Services. The net reserve build of $973 million included $549 million in Wholesale and $441 million in Consumer and was largely driven by changes in the weighted-average macroeconomic outlook. The prior-year provision was $1.9 billion, net charge-offs were $2.0 billion and the net reserve release was $72 million.
  • Shares +2%.
